name: Route of the Faces
name_original: Ruta de la Caras – running circular desde Buendía
description: Jog the Ruta de la Caras, a 7.7-mile route from Buendía, featuring unique rock sculptures and reservoir views through pine forests.
region_intro: Jogging around Buendía offers diverse natural features within the Alcarria de Cuenca region of Spain. The area presents an open landscape of the Spanish plateau, characterized by steppes, grasslands, and extensive pine forests. The Guadiela River and the Buendía Dam create tranquil environments with scenic views, providing varied terrain for running. Best jogging routes around Buendía The most popular jogging route is Route of the Faces, a 7.7 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete, leading through pine forests with unique carved sculptures. Another top favourite among local runners is Virgen del Socorro Trail, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path that features varied terrain and elevation changes. Local runners also love the Running loop from Valdeconcha, a 3.8 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the open landscape, often completed in about 48 minutes. Jogging around Buendía is defined by riverine environments, extensive pine forests, and open grasslands. The network offers options for moderate and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Buendía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Buendía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Buendía, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6 dedicated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Buendía. The majority are classified as &lt;em&gt;moderate&lt;/em&gt;, with 5 routes falling into this category. There is also 1 &lt;em&gt;difficult&lt;/em&gt; route for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Buendía?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; offer a unique experience with allegorical sculptures carved into sandstone, which can be engaging for families. The terrain around the Buendía Dam and reservoir also provides flatter paths suitable for a more relaxed pace.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Buendía?
A: Buendía's diverse natural landscapes, including pine forests and open grasslands, generally welcome dogs. Many trails, especially those through natural areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, are suitable for running with dogs. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any local regulations regarding pets in specific areas.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Buendía area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31912681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Valdeconcha&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.8-mile (6.0 km) circular trail through open landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces – The Route of the Faces loop from Buendía&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 10.5 km circular experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Buendía?
A: Buendía, located on the Spanish plateau, experiences varied seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, with comfortable weather and beautiful natural scenery. Summer can be warm, but routes through pine forests, such as those near the Buendía lake, provide welcome shade. Winter runs are also possible, but it's advisable to check weather conditions and dress appropriately.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and unique attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; is famous for its allegorical sculptures carved into sandstone rocks. You can also encounter the tranquil waters of the Guadiela River and the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; and reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt;, located by the Guadiela River, is another notable spot with amenities for a rest.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Buendía?
A: The running routes in Buendía are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Runners often praise the diverse natural features, from riverine environments and extensive pine forests to open grasslands, which provide varied and visually rewarding experiences.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation changes for a more challenging run?
A: Yes, for those looking for more challenging terrain, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031416599/virgen-del-socorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Socorro Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.5-mile (7.3 km) path featuring varied terrain and notable elevation changes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sacedón&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, with over 600 meters of elevation gain, offering a substantial workout.

Q: Where can I park when accessing the running trails in Buendía?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, a popular starting point, typically has designated parking areas nearby due to its popularity as a hiking and running destination. For other routes, parking is generally available in or near the closest towns like Buendía or Valdeconcha.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Guadiela River or the Buendía Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Guadiela River and the Buendía Dam and Reservoir are prominent natural features in the region. Many routes will offer views of these bodies of water, and some paths likely run along their banks, providing tranquil environments with stunning backdrops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt; is situated directly by the Guadiela River, suggesting nearby running opportunities.

Q: What is the longest running route available in the Buendía area?
A: The longest route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sacedón&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 9.5 miles (15.4 km) and is considered a difficult trail due to its length and elevation gain.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through pine forests?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts vast, young pine forests, particularly near the Buendía lake in an area known as The Peninsula. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through these pine forests, offering shade, fresh air, and a serene natural setting for your run.
tips:
Free access tourist route located near the municipality of Buendía, Cuenca, among pine forests, sandstone stones abound. Here you will find the Ruta de las Caras, a hiking route of a couple of kilometers in which you will be accompanied by pine trees and allegorical sculptures.
Faces sculpted in sandstone rock of different sizes, some spectacular, with dimensions of four and six meters, which took eight years to sculpt due to the hardness of the rock.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: The full circular running route from Buendía, as mapped on komoot, typically takes around 1 hour and 28 minutes to complete. However, the core section of The Route of the Faces itself is shorter, usually taking 45 to 60 minutes to walk or jog.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this running route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate for jogging. While the core Ruta de las Caras section is often described as a 'simple route' suitable for all ages, the natural surfaces and presence of numerous sculptures might encourage frequent stops. The overall circular route from Buendía involves some uneven sections, making it less ideal for continuous, high-speed running but still very manageable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta de la Caras?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking near the entrance to the Ruta de las Caras, which is easily accessible from Buendía. The trail is well-signposted from the town.

faqs.question: What unique features can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The most distinctive feature is The Route of the Faces, an open-air museum where over 15 to 20 sculptures and bas-reliefs are carved directly into sandstone rocks. You'll encounter figures like 'La Monja' (The Nun), 'Krishna', 'Maitreya', and even a face of Beethoven, all set amidst a 'petrified world' of ancient rocks and pine forests.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for running?
faqs.answer: The terrain consists primarily of natural surfaces, often featuring large sandstone rocks. The path winds through serene pine forests, offering plenty of shade, and provides picturesque views of the Buendía reservoir. You might also notice aromatic plants like lavender, sage, and rosemary.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. The pine forests provide good shade, making it viable even on warmer days, but avoiding the peak heat of summer midday is advisable.

faqs.question: Is this running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta de las Caras is generally considered dog-friendly. Always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to respect other visitors and the natural environment.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Ruta de las Caras or to run this circular route from Buendía. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like picnic areas available?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are picnic areas available at the entrance to the Ruta de las Caras, which can be a great spot to relax before or after your run.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular running route that starts and ends in Buendía, incorporating the famous Ruta de las Caras.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for running this route?
faqs.answer: Given the natural and sometimes uneven terrain, wear comfortable running shoes with good grip. Bring water, especially on warmer days, and consider a light backpack for essentials. Sun protection is also recommended, particularly for sections not under tree cover.
